assertions.assertion: A SKILL.md file exists in the output skill directory
The SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description fields
assertions.evidence: Lines 1-4 contain --- delimited YAML frontmatter with 'name: git-commit-writer' and a 'description:' field

assertions.assertion: The name field uses only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens
The description includes 'Use when' trigger conditions
assertions.evidence: Description on line 3 contains 'Use when committing code, writing commit messages, preparing git commits, reviewing staged changes, or when the user says "commit this" or "write a commit message"'

assertions.assertion: The SKILL.md body contains a numbered workflow or step-by-step instructions
assertions.evidence: Body contains 8 numbered steps: '### 1. Gather the staged diff' through '### 8. Present and confirm'

assertions.assertion: A scripts/ directory exists with at least one Bun TypeScript script (.ts file)
assertions.evidence: scripts/parse-diff.ts exists (394 lines), uses '#!/usr/bin/env bun' shebang and imports from 'bun'

assertions.assertion: The script includes a --help flag or usage documentation
assertions.evidence: Script checks args.includes('--help') on line 67 and prints usage documentation including options and output format

assertions.assertion: An evals/evals.json file exists with at least 2 test cases
assertions.evidence: evals/evals.json exists with 3 test cases (ids 1, 2, 3) covering simple feature, monorepo feature, and breaking change scenarios

assertions.assertion: The SKILL.md body includes a Common mistakes or pitfalls section
assertions.evidence: Line 263: '## Common mistakes' section with an 11-row table covering imperative mood, subject length, wrong type, file paths as scope, missing BREAKING CHANGE, etc.

assertions.assertion: The SKILL.md body is under 500 lines
assertions.evidence: SKILL.md is 323 lines total, well under the 500-line limit

assertions.assertion: The script uses structured JSON output to stdout
assertions.evidence: Line 394: console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2)) outputs a structured ParseResult object with files, summary, monorepo, suggested_type, and suggested_scope fields
